title: New-grad job hunt — applications to a signed offer
description: Two live applications: Stripe (applied, top choice, referral in play) and Cloudflare (interviewing, Austin hybrid). Goal is one signed offer without torching the current Datadog seat — internal mobility stays a fallback. Children carry the stages.

title: Complete the Cloudflare technical screen
description: First real interview of the hunt: a 60-minute screen, likely two coding exercises plus a short systems question. Warm up on the practice set the week before.
close_reason: Screen passed — two coding exercises and a systems question on rate limiting. Recruiter confirmed advancement to the final-round virtual onsite the same afternoon; scheduling for the week of July 20.

comments.author: coach
comments.text: Ran a timed mock the night before: both practice problems solved inside the window, and the rate-limiter warm-up turned out to be the exact topic the screen opened with.

title: Prep the Cloudflare final round — systems design + behavioral
description: Virtual onsite the week of July 20: one systems-design session, one deeper coding round, one behavioral. Plan: two mock design sessions (URL shortener, log pipeline) and five STAR stories drawn from the internship and the current Datadog project work.

title: Negotiate the offer if the final round converts
description: Posted band is $120k–$150k. Anchor in the top half, cite the live Stripe application as the competing process, and never accept on the call — ask for the offer in writing and 48 hours.

title: Nudge the Stripe application through the referral
description: Applied June 17 via a bootcamp friend's referral; three weeks of silence is long enough. One polite nudge through the referrer, then let it ride.
close_reason: Referrer pinged their recruiting contact — the application is marked under review and a recruiter screen is expected within two weeks. Nothing more to do until they reach out.

title: Emergency fund to three months, then automate investing
description: The HYSA sits at $5.4k against a ~$9k target (three months of Austin rent plus expenses). Order of operations: automate the savings transfer, fill the buffer, and only then switch on the taxable auto-invest. The Roth contribution cadence rides alongside.

title: Set up the automatic $500/mo transfer to the HYSA
description: Automate before optimizing: $250 out of each paycheck, scheduled the day after payday so it never bounces.
close_reason: Recurring transfer is live — $250 from each paycheck lands the day after payday. First transfer confirmed July 10; the checking-account floor held with room to spare.

comments.author: coach
comments.text: At $500/mo from a $5.4k base, the $9k target lands around February — sooner if any tax refund or vest proceeds top it up.

title: Grow the emergency fund to $9k — three months of expenses
description: The tracking task the automation feeds. Rule: the buffer only ever goes up until it hits target — vest proceeds and windfalls can accelerate it, nothing withdraws from it.

title: Turn on the $300/mo auto-invest into VTI
description: Buffer first, market second: the auto-invest starts the month the HYSA crosses $9k. Keep the taxable core boring — the money goes to VTI, and the two-fund index core stays the whole strategy.

title: Set the 2026 Roth IRA contribution schedule
description: The Roth holds $7.2k with no recurring contribution yet. Map a monthly amount that finishes the year at a sensible total without crowding the emergency-fund cadence — the target-date fund inside it needs no decisions.

title: First solo apartment — search to signed lease
description: The shared lease ends September 30 — first solo place, hard cap $1,500/mo so the savings plan survives the rent increase. Sequence: shortlist neighborhoods, tour, sign, move.

title: Shortlist three Austin neighborhoods under the rent cap
description: Filter on three things only: one-bedrooms posting under $1,500, a tolerable commute, and enough listing volume that losing one unit isn't losing the neighborhood.
close_reason: Shortlist locked: East Riverside, North Loop, and Crestview. All three have one-bedrooms currently posting under the cap and a commute under 30 minutes.

comments.author: scout
comments.text: Pulled 30 days of listing history: North Loop has the thinnest sub-$1,500 inventory of the three, so move fast if a unit shows there. East Riverside has the most churn and the most leverage.

title: Tour the shortlisted units
description: Six tours booked across the three neighborhoods over the next two weekends. Score each unit on the same four axes — light, noise, in-unit laundry, commute — so the decision is a comparison, not a vibe.

title: Sign the lease + schedule the move
description: Deposit plus first month runs roughly $3k out of pocket — that comes from cash flow, not the emergency fund. Target a September 1 start so the overlap with the shared lease stays under a month.

title: Read up on SCHD before adding a dividend tilt
description: It's on the watchlist, but a dividend tilt at this account size probably just loses to the plain index after taxes. An hour of reading before any buy — the point is to understand it, not to own it.

title: Price flights for the August Denver weekend
description: AUS–DEN, August 14–17, visiting college friends and crashing on a couch — the flight is the entire trip budget. Anything under $150 round trip is a book-it price.

title: Plan the September DDOG vest — sell and reinvest per the playbook
description: The next RSU vest lands September 15. Same playbook as June: sell the vested lot, roll the proceeds into VTI, keep the employer concentration flat. Nothing to do until the vest window nears.
